projects.title: Unit 7 Project — Equations & Inequalities in Action
projects.desc: Capstone project applying writing and solving equations and inequalities to real situations.

projects.title: Neon Inequality Gatekeeper
projects.desc: Solve and graph inequalities on a number line to clear each gate.

projects.title: One-Step Solver Arena
projects.desc: Practice the one-step solving moves behind inequality solutions.

title: Solve and Graph Inequalities
contentObjective: I can solve an inequality and graph its solution set on a number line.
languageObjective: I can explain my work using the words solve, graph, solution, and substitute.

turnAndTalk.question: Solving an inequality and solving an equation both isolate the variable. What is the same about your steps, and what is different about the answer you get?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Solving an inequality gives a range of solutions, not one number.

stems.en: The steps are the same because I ___ to ___ the variable.
stems.es: Los pasos son iguales porque ___ para ___ la variable.

stems.en: The answer is different because an inequality has ___.
stems.es: La respuesta es diferente porque una desigualdad tiene ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students note both use inverse operations to isolate the variable, but an inequality's solution is many values (a range) while an equation's is a single value.
turnAndTalk.extend: Predict: after you solve an inequality, how will the graph look different from graphing the solution to an equation? Justify.
extendStems: The inequality graph shows ___ while an equation shows ___.

turnAndTalk.question: What steps did you follow to solve and then graph the inequality, and how did you check a value to make sure your solution set is right?
turnAndTalk.kernel: I solve to isolate the variable, then substitute a value to test the solution set.

stems.en: First I solved by ___, then I graphed ___.
I substituted ___ to check that it ___ the inequality.
stems.es: Sustituí ___ para verificar que ___ la desigualdad.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students isolate the variable, graph the solution set with the correct circle and shading, and substitute a test value to confirm it makes the inequality true.
turnAndTalk.extend: If you substitute a value from the shaded region and it makes the inequality false, what does that tell you about your solution? Explain.
extendStems: If a shaded value makes it false, then I ___.
A correct solution set means every shaded value ___.

turnAndTalk.question: When would solving an inequality help you stay within a real-life limit?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Inequalities describe rules where many amounts are allowed up to a limit.

stems.en: Solving an inequality would help me stay within ___ when ___.
stems.es: Resolver una desigualdad me ayudaría a quedarme dentro de ___ cuando ___.

stems.en: I would substitute ___ to check I am within the limit.
stems.es: Sustituiría ___ para verificar que estoy dentro del límite.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students give a real budget/time/capacity limit and explain that the inequality's solution set shows all amounts that keep them within the limit.
turnAndTalk.extend: Critique: 'The solution to an inequality is just like the solution to an equation — one number.' Use a solved example to explain why this is wrong.
extendStems: This is wrong because an inequality solution is ___.
For example, solving ___ gives all values that ___.

vocabulary.definition: To find the number that makes it true.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Encontrar el número que la hace verdadera.
vocabulary.visual: x + 3 > 10 → subtract 3 → x > 7 — any number greater than 7 works
vocabulary.cloze: To find all the values that make an inequality true is to ___ it.

vocabulary.definition: To show answers on a number line with circles and shading.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Mostrar respuestas en una recta numérica con círculos y sombreado.
vocabulary.visual: x > 7: open circle at 7, shade right — shows all solutions at a glance
vocabulary.cloze: To show the solutions on a number line is to ___ them.

vocabulary.definition: A number that makes the equation or inequality true.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un número que hace verdadera la ecuación o desigualdad.
vocabulary.visual: x = 8 is a solution to x > 7 because 8 > 7 is true; x = 5 is not because 5 > 7 is false
vocabulary.cloze: A value that makes an inequality true is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: To put a number in for the letter to check if it works.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Poner un número en lugar de la letra para ver si funciona.
vocabulary.visual: Substitute x = 5 into x + 3 > 10: 5 + 3 = 8, and 8 > 10 is false, so x = 5 is not a solution
vocabulary.cloze: To check a solution, I ___ the value back into the inequality.

vocabulary.definition: All of the values that make an inequality true.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Todos los valores que hacen verdadera una desigualdad.
vocabulary.visual: For x > 4, the solution set is every number greater than 4, such as 5, 6, 7, ...
vocabulary.cloze: All the values that make an inequality true form the ___.

vocabulary.definition: An operation that undoes another, used to isolate the variable.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una operación que deshace otra, usada para despejar la variable.
vocabulary.visual: To solve x + 3 > 7, use the inverse of adding 3: subtract 3 to get x > 4.
vocabulary.cloze: An operation that undoes another to isolate the variable is an ___.

launch.badge: Case File: Solve and Map It
launch.narrative: Detective Kim knows that the suspect withdrew more than $50 from an ATM. The amount plus a $3 fee totaled more than $53. She writes x + 3 > 53 and needs to solve for x, then graph the solution set to share with the bank. How can she find and display all possible withdrawal amounts?
launch.contextImage: Detective at a desk with an ATM receipt, calculator, and a number line drawn on a whiteboard
noticePrompts: What is the unknown in this situation?
What operation do you need to undo?
Will the answer be one number or many numbers?
wonderPrompts: What if the total was at most $53 instead of more than $53?
How would the graph change if the symbol were ≥ instead of >?

conceptIntro.heading: How do I solve an inequality and then graph it?
conceptIntro.intro: I solve an inequality the same way as an equation: use inverse operations to get the variable alone. But the answer is a range of values, so I show all of them on a number line.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: Solve with inverse operations, then graph the solution set: open circle for < or >, closed for ≤ or ≥.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: Solve x + 3 > 53. The inverse of adding 3 is subtracting 3.
I subtract 3 from both sides: x + 3 − 3 > 53 − 3, so x > 50.
The symbol > does not include 50, so I use an open circle at 50.
'Greater than' shades right. Check: x = 60 gives 60 + 3 = 63 > 53. True!

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: Solve x − 5 ≤ 8. What is the inverse of subtracting 5?
We add 5 to both sides: x ≤ 8 + 5, so x ≤ 13.
≤ includes 13, so we use a closed circle and shade left.

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: Solve and graph x + 4 > 10, x − 5 ≤ 8, and x + 7 ≥ 15 on the number line.
Then finish the practice problems and test a value to check.

explore.instructions: Solve each inequality using inverse operations, then graph the solution on the number line.
hints: First solve the inequality just like an equation — use inverse operations on both sides. Then graph the result.
Remember: open circle for < and >, closed circle for ≤ and ≥. Shade in the direction of the solution values.
explore.label: Solve each inequality, then graph the solution on the number line.

discourse.prompt: How is solving an inequality similar to and different from solving an equation?
discourse.sentenceFrame: Solving an inequality is like solving an equation because I still use ___, but it is different because the answer is ___ instead of ___.

optional.stem: Clue 1: Which value is a solution of x > 7?
optional.explanation: A solution makes x > 7 true. Substitute each: 9 > 7 is true, but 7 > 7, 5 > 7, and 3 > 7 are all false. So 9 is the solution that opens the gate.

optional.stem: Clue 2: Is 5 a solution of x ≤ 5?
choices: No, because 5 is not less than 5
No, because ≤ means only smaller numbers
Yes, but only because 5 is odd
optional.explanation: Substitute x = 5 into x ≤ 5: 5 ≤ 5 is true because ≤ means 'less than OR equal to.' The boundary value 5 IS a solution, so the gate opens.

optional.instructions: Detective, sort each suspect's badge number into the right vault door for the rule x ≥ 4. A value is a solution if it makes x ≥ 4 true.

optional.label: Lock-and-key match: connect each badge number to the one inequality it is a solution of. Substitute to check that each value makes its inequality true.

optional.stem: Final gate — describe the solution set. The vault rule is x > 7. What does the solution set mean?
choices: All numbers greater than 7, like 8, 9, 10, … (but NOT 7 itself)
optional.explanation: The solution set is every value that makes x > 7 true. That means all numbers greater than 7, such as 8, 9, 10, and beyond. Because > does not include the boundary, 7 itself is NOT in the solution set.

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — Catch the Boundary Mistake

workedExample.label: Rookie's conclusion
workedExample.work: 7 > 7 is true, so 7 IS a solution and opens the gate.

workedExample.label: Mark the solution set
workedExample.work: The detective adds 7 to the list of solutions.

optional.correctWork: Substitute x = 7 into x > 7: this asks if 7 is greater than 7. It is NOT — 7 equals 7, it is not greater. So 7 > 7 is false, which means 7 is NOT a solution. Because the symbol is > (not ≥), the boundary value 7 is left out of the solution set. The solution set is all numbers strictly greater than 7, such as 8 and up.
hints: Substitute x = 7 into x > 7 carefully: does 7 > 7? A number cannot be greater than itself.
Remember the difference between > and ≥: only ≥ includes the boundary value. With >, the boundary is NOT a solution.

approaching.explanation: Subtract 6 from both sides: x > 14 − 6 = 8. Graph: open circle at 8, shade right.
Add 9 to both sides: x ≤ 3 + 9 = 12. Graph: closed circle at 12, shade left.
Subtract 10 from both sides: x ≥ 25 − 10 = 15. Graph: closed circle at 15, shade right.
Add 4 to both sides: x < 11 + 4 = 15. Graph: open circle at 15, shade left.

approaching.instructions: Solve x + 3 > 9, then graph the solution on the number line.
hints: Open circle at 6, shade to the right.

onLevel.label: Solve each inequality, describe the inverse operation, and describe the graph.

onLevel.stem: Is x = 5 a solution to x + 4 > 12?
choices: No, because 5 + 4 = 9, and 9 is not greater than 12
onLevel.explanation: Substitute: 5 + 4 = 9. Since 9 > 12 is false, x = 5 is NOT a solution.

onLevel.instructions: Solve each inequality first, then sort by whether x = 10 is a solution.
hints: First solve the inequality to find the solution set. Then check if 10 is in that set.
For x > 12: is 10 > 12? No. For x > 6: is 10 > 6? Yes.
onLevel.label: Solve each inequality, then test if x = 10 is a solution.
Solve the inequality x + 7 ≥ 19 using the balance scale. What is the boundary value?
hints: The boundary is 12. x can be 12 or any number greater than 12.

extending.title: Find the Solving Error
extending.correctWork: The inverse of subtracting 8 is ADDING 8, not subtracting. Add 8 to both sides: x ≥ 15 + 8 = 23. Graph: closed circle at 23, shade right.
hints: The equation has − 8. What is the inverse of subtracting 8?
To undo − 8, you add 8 to both sides: x ≥ 15 + 8 = 23.

extending.prompt: A detective has a budget of at most $100 for supplies. She already spent $37 on gloves. Write an inequality for the remaining amount r she can spend, solve it, graph it, and name three possible values for r.
extending.sentenceFrame: Inequality: 37 + r ≤ ___. Solving: r ≤ ___ − 37 = ___. Graph: ___ circle at ___, shade ___. Three possible values: ___.

extending.label: Solve each inequality and verify by testing two values.

optionalActivity.name: Inequality Escape Path
optionalActivity.intro: Detective, each value is a clue — only true solutions clear the next gate and lead you out of the vault.

connect.scenario: A detective needs at least $75 for a surveillance camera. She has already saved $48. The inequality 48 + s ≥ 75 represents how much more money s she needs.
connect.promptQuestion: How much more does the detective need? How did you solve and graph this inequality?
connect.prompt: The detective needs at least $__ more because 48 + s ≥ 75 means s ≥ 75 − ___ = ___.

exitTicket.stem: Solve and describe the graph: x + 5 < 11
choices: x < 6; open circle at 6, shade left
x < 16; open circle at 16, shade left
x ≤ 6; closed circle at 6, shade left
x > 6; open circle at 6, shade right
exitTicket.explanation: Subtract 5 from both sides: x < 11 − 5 = 6. Open circle at 6 (not included), shade left. ✓
